title: Course module outline (arabic-authoring-suite)
description: Schema for an Arabic course module (technical/opinion register). Differs from article and book chapter in three structural ways: (1) requires learning_objectives at the top level; (2) requires exercises with answer keys; (3) sections include a 'pedagogical_role' tag (intro/concept/example/exercise/synthesis) used by the drafter to vary tone and structure across sections.

items.description: Bloom-style objective: 'By the end of this module, the learner will be able to ...'. The drafter checks each section against the objective list at humanizer-gate time.

prerequisites.description: Prior knowledge assumed. Empty array is allowed for intro-level modules.

exercises.description: Minimum 3 exercises with answer keys. Drafted alongside the module body, not after.

key_terms.description: Glossary entries cross-referenced into the prose. Sourced from arabic-corpus-toolkit dictionary when available; module-specific terms added inline.

pedagogical_role.description: Drives tone variance: 'concept' sections use technical register strictly; 'example' sections relax tone slightly to support comprehension. The humanness-gate threshold (50) is calibrated for the technical register and tolerates this variance.

source_refs.description: Course modules tolerate sections with empty source_refs ONLY for pedagogical_role='exercise' or 'intro'. Concept and synthesis sections must cite.

covers_objectives.description: Indices into learning_objectives that this section addresses. Drafter validates that every objective is covered by at least one section.

answer_key_ar.description: Required. Exercises without answer keys are scaffolding holes — the schema rejects them.
